[CPE Webinar] Audit Methodology That Works: Making Your Process
 Practical and Powerful
body:
Can't view this message? Click Here <https://app.teammatesolutions.com/e/es?s=2178&e=1181234&elqTrackId=1cea87cdefb44f3684ad1df2f8e797e1&elq=d68fe6528a93483d84e04581e211d051&elqaid=6721&elqat=1&elqak=8AF535717E45AAD21F417D2FFE30F0C11162DC6AF1F328F05025215D87E6781975A8>


Hi Hammad,

A strong methodology is the backbone of any successful internal audit function — but too often it’s seen as bureaucratic or overly rigid, focused on compliance with standards rather than efficient delivery of organisational value.

This session takes a fresh look at what makes an audit methodology work in practice. We will break down the essential elements: risk assessment, planning, fieldwork, reporting, and follow-up and in the context of the Global Internal Audit Standards, explore how to keep them proportionate, efficient, and value-driven.

Join us on Tuesday, 7 July, for a practical and insightful live webinar led by Jonathan Chapman. Walk away with ideas on how to simplify their methodology, embed good practice, and ensure consistency across the function.

Audit Methodology That Works: Making Your Process Practical and Powerful 

Tuesday, 7 July 2026 | 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM BST

Learning Objectives:
*Understand the essential components of an effective audit methodology
*Design an audit manual that works in practice
*Assess the growing regulatory and governance expectations and understand internal audit's role in providing assurance in these areas.
*Apply the methodology across the full audit lifecycle
*Simplify and standardize their audit activity for efficiency and consistency
*Use the methodology to drive value-led insights and impact

Speaker: Jonathan Chapman | Internal Audit Strategy Adviser | Former Audit COO, Aviva & Lloyds Banking Group

Hi Hammad,

Internal audit leaders are raising the bar for what they expect from audit management software — and for good reason.

As audit teams move away from spreadsheets and disconnected tools, they’re looking for platforms that support risk-based assurance, consistent execution, and visibility across the full audit lifecycle, from planning and fieldwork to reporting and follow-up.

According to the Gartner® Market Guide for Audit Management Software <https://app.teammatesolutions.com/e/er?utm_campaign=cpesg_tm_email_What%20Gartner%20Says%20Audit%20Leaders%20Need%20in%202026&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&s=2178&lid=4918&elqTrackId=992159B42DE045ADDF2EE17D01D988A2&elq=892c77d077ca47bf80084bb5d91d828b&elqaid=6727&elqat=1&elqak=8AF53DE2D9D6477E47601682CE7F20214990723D6CFBBE3E3C868529E826CD7446BE>, we see that audit teams are prioritizing:

*Strong support for audit workflows including planning, execution, reporting, and issue follow-up
*Intuitive user experiences that support — not constrain — audit teams
*Audit-specific capabilities beyond broader GRC platforms
*Practical AI capabilities that improve efficiency without compromising audit quality

We believe TeamMate Audit aligns with these evolving expectations through purpose-built capabilities for risk-based planning, structured audit execution, issue tracking, and audit performance visibility — all while maintaining an audit-first focus.

subject: [CPE Webinar] The  GIAA’s  Path to AI-Driven Auditing
body:
Can't view this message? Click Here <https://app.teammatesolutions.com/e/es?s=2178&e=1178143&elqTrackId=1cea87cdefb44f3684ad1df2f8e797e1&elq=27bea8e93e134d2e818c705824542071&elqaid=6724&elqat=1&elqak=8AF59B86C1C9CCD1A5552605011D5D265FEF233CB42B820E07EE0593A637324E2499>


Hi Hammad,

This presentation will explore the UK Government Internal Audit Agency’s (GIAA) journey in integrating artificial intelligence into everyday audit work. Conducting around 1,500 internal audits annually, GIAA has leveraged AI to reduce the time required for some audit activities. We will discuss how GIAA progressed from using AI to extract insights from its extensive library of internal audit reports, to enhancing risk articulation, and even drafting audit reports in under 30 seconds.

Join us on Wednesday, 17 June, for a practical and insightful live webinar led by Dr Iain McGregor. The session will share valuable lessons learned in staff adoption and strategies used to encourage widespread use of AI. 

Finally, we will delve into GIAA’s pioneering research into autonomous AI capabilities to analyse documents, assess control environment design, test control effectiveness, and generate reports with human oversight. This transformational approach promises to reshape the future auditor’s role, audit management systems, and professional standards.

From Insight to Autonomy: The GIAA’s Path to AI-Driven Auditing
Wednesday, 17 June 2026 | 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM BST
Speaker:  Dr Iain McGregor | Director of Innovation, Government Internal Audit Agency

Learning Objectives:
*Understand the practical applications of AI in internal audit processes at GIAA.
*Explore how AI can reduce audit cycle times and improve efficiency.
*Learn about the challenges and strategies involved in promoting AI adoption among audit staff.
*Gain insights into the emerging use of autonomous AI for control environment assessment and reporting.
*Discover the potential impacts of AI on the future skills, roles, and standards in internal auditing.

CYBERSECURITY

Cybersecurity and digital resilience: The 2026 outlook for governance

Cybersecurity is no longer just an IT issue—it’s a critical board-level business risk. With cybersecurity threats growing more sophisticated, driven by geopolitical tensions and rapid technological advancements like artificial intelligence, organizations are under immense pressure to stay ahead. The stakes are high, and the role of internal audit in this evolving landscape has never been more important.

Read More → <https://app.teammatesolutions.com/e/er?utm_campaign=cpesg_tm_email_AI%20generated%20audit%20evidence%2C%20Cybersecurity%20and%20digital%20resilience%20%282026%20outlook%29%2C%20and%20more.&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&s=2178&lid=4920&elqTrackId=6D841B8E71A579C3C09EBF6C690C2FD8&elq=127bb9e2c6be42e0831f73ebec0a304e&elqaid=6728&elqat=1&elqak=8AF5784AF80D30E0B156C39639C504D516E3E8CD089D500889F6D06BC154A7CB3FF6>

Data privacy vs. data security: What internal auditors need to know

Think of that highly secure bank vault the protagonists from your favorite heist movie encountered. Steel doors, biometric locks, 24/7 surveillance, counterweights, the works. That is top-tier security. But what happens if the bank tellers are loudly repeating customers’ account balances and Social Security numbers across the lobby? The vault doesn’t matter anymore. The bank has a massive privacy problem.

SPEAKERS:

Sandy Buchanan, CISSP, CISM, CISA, GSCA

Lead Product Manager, Wolters Kluwer TeamMate

Michael Trpkosh, MBA, CISSP, CISA, CISM

Co-Founder & Chief Information Security Officer, Bolder Cyber Partners

Hi Hammad,

Modern identity governance is about more than managing user accounts. The rapid expansion of identities beyond human users to include service accounts, machine identities, workload identities, and autonomous AI agents has outstripped the capabilities of most traditional IAM programs. This webinar explores how this expanded attack surface has resulted in critical gaps in attribution, privilege enforcement, and compliance, and examines the four pillars of effective identity governance:

*Inventory
*Observability
*Accountability
*Governance

Attendees will leave with a clearer understanding of the risks posed by ungoverned non-human identities and concrete actions they can take to adapt their governance models to mitigate these risks in their environments.

Learning Objectives:
*Learn the five major identity types operating in modern environments (human, non-human, machine, workload, and agentic) and the different governance approaches required by each.
*Understand how poorly governed non-human identities create real security and compliance risk, including attribution failures during incidents, orphaned credentials, and blind spots in traditional architectures.
*Take away a four-pillar framework for identity governance that you can use to assess and address gaps in your own environment.
*Identify a set of practical first steps for extending governance beyond human users, including credential lifecycle management and foundational controls for non-human identities.

Join us Thursday, June 18, 2026, at 11:00 AM EDT for a free 1-hour webinar. Participants will earn 1 CPE credit.

We hope to see you there!

SPEAKER:

John Hall

President, Hall Consulting, Inc.

Hi Hammad,

Audit testing, data analytics and now AI all help us bring forward issues that need management attention. But despite these powerful tools, auditing very much remains a human endeavor. And effective presentation of observations, findings, advisory suggestions, and recommendations comes down to the ability of the auditor and any change leader to verbally present a compelling business case that drives action.

In this program, we’ll explore how to use our presentation behavior skills to capture and hold management’s attention. We’ll cover exactly what works and what to avoid, and how to increase the probability of effective management response through relevant social proof examples.

Learning Objectives:
*Explore how to improve verbal presentation skills so we’re understood and followed.
*Identify how to use our soft skills to make our ideas ‘stick’ as a first step in influencing action.
*Recognize the incredible influencing power of Social Proof examples during presentations.
*Identify what works and what gets in the way during our presentations.

Join us Tuesday, June 9, 2026, at 11:00 AM EDT for a free 1-hour webinar. Participants will earn 1 CPE credit.

We hope to see you there!

Hi Hammad,
The internal audit profession is navigating one of the most significant periods of workforce disruption in its history. 

This webinar brings together the strategic, operational, and assurance dimensions of this transformation. Whether you are a Chief Audit Executive designing a future-fit team, a risk professional advising on culture and ethics, or an audit committee seeking to strengthen your oversight role, this session will equip you with practical insight into the challenges reshaping the profession—and the opportunities they present.

Join us on Thursday, 25 June, for a practical and insightful live webinar led by Liz Sandwith. The session is designed for internal auditors and audit committee members as she explores the profound workforce challenges facing internal audit functions.

Talent, Culture & Workforce Transformation in Internal Audit | Thursday, 25 June 2026 | 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM BST
Speaker: Liz Sandwith, Internal Audit and Risk Consultant, member of the IIA Professional Certification Board, and former Member of the IIA Standards Board

Learning Objectives:
Identify the key talent pressures facing internal audit functions today.
Evaluate the impact of post-pandemic workforce trends — including burnout, attrition and shifting employee expectations.
Assess the growing regulatory and governance expectations and understand internal audit's role in providing assurance in these areas.
Apply an audit and risk lens to culture and succession.
Define the evolving role of the audit committee in talent oversight and culture audits.

Join us Thursday, 25 June 2026, at 10:00 AM BST for a free 1-hour webinar. Participants will earn 1 CPE credit.

subject: [CPE Webinar] The  GIAA’s  Path to AI-Driven Auditing
body:
Can't view this message? Click Here <https://app.teammatesolutions.com/e/es?s=2178&e=1173362&elqTrackId=1cea87cdefb44f3684ad1df2f8e797e1&elq=3124b01081a84c978ab703df3406b9be&elqaid=6691&elqat=1&elqak=8AF57D79C08DBFB5D66557B587919A8C1B53875A9FCCD21795624CBBE9408E7EA60A>


Hi Hammad,

This presentation will explore the UK Government Internal Audit Agency’s (GIAA) journey in integrating artificial intelligence into everyday audit work. Conducting around 1,500 internal audits annually, GIAA has leveraged AI to reduce the time required for some audit activities. We will discuss how GIAA progressed from using AI to extract insights from its extensive library of internal audit reports, to enhancing risk articulation, and even drafting audit reports in under 30 seconds.

Join us on Wednesday, 17 June, for a practical and insightful live webinar led by Dr Iain McGregor. The session will share valuable lessons learned in staff adoption and strategies used to encourage widespread use of AI. 

Finally, we will delve into GIAA’s pioneering research into autonomous AI capabilities to analyse documents, assess control environment design, test control effectiveness, and generate reports with human oversight. This transformational approach promises to reshape the future auditor’s role, audit management systems, and professional standards.

From Insight to Autonomy: The GIAA’s Path to AI-Driven Auditing

Wednesday, 17 June 2026 | 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM BST
                                                  
Speaker:  Dr Iain McGregor | Director of Innovation, Government Internal Audit Agency

Learning Objectives:
*Understand the practical applications of AI in internal audit processes at GIAA.
*Explore how AI can reduce audit cycle times and improve efficiency.
*Learn about the challenges and strategies involved in promoting AI adoption among audit staff.
*Gain insights into the emerging use of autonomous AI for control environment assessment and reporting.
*Discover the potential impacts of AI on the future skills, roles, and standards in internal auditing.

digital growth and performance-driven marketing solutions for businesses.

and optimizing paid advertising campaigns for lead generation and

Shopping, Performance Max, and Demand Gen campaigns focused on improving

campaigns

Hi Hammad,

You've probably heard of agile, but what is it, actually? It's an essential mindset and set of methods that equip internal auditors to protect their enterprise in an environment of rapidly emerging new technologies and new threats. This session will clarify fundamental Agile concepts, dispel common misconceptions, and give participants several proven techniques they can begin using immediately to increase the business impact of their audits.

*Explain the four core values of the agile mindset and use them to guide decisions about how their internal audit function could deliver greater business value.

*Formulate answers to the three core questions in a project canvas, a tool designed to foster greater focus on business value.
*Implement elements of core agile concepts, including servant leadership, and identify symptoms of “bad agile” and learn how to avoid it.
*Begin using a visual display, a.k.a. “Kanban Board”, and understand how TeamMate enables this agile concept.

Register → <https://tm.wolterskluwer.com/LP=1901?utm_campaign=cpesg_tm_email_Building%20a%20practical%20AI%20governance%20and%20audit%20framework&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&src=Email&cid=701Ph000019TVM6IAO&elqTrackId=47EBD782FC720FCB71D3DAC3E8AC0CEC&elq=23a02631a3ad4610b05910997341f9f9&elqaid=6658&elqat=1&elqCampaignId=8405&elqak=8AF591FA415E4F2FFBC2ECEDEECB9A1094E8E25199865B901F712BE2F3AFEA409CC3>                      

<https://tm.wolterskluwer.com/LP=1901?utm_campaign=cpesg_tm_email_Building%20a%20practical%20AI%20governance%20and%20audit%20framework&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&src=Email&cid=701Ph000019TVM6IAO&elqTrackId=948F1225A3B66A24F928EBB806062F2F&elq=23a02631a3ad4610b05910997341f9f9&elqaid=6658&elqat=1&elqCampaignId=8405&elqak=8AF52197212AE223ABD20470C204C333C8C9E25199865B901F712BE2F3AFEA409CC3>                                                               

SPEAKER:

Mike Levy, CIA, CRMA, CISSP, CISA, CDPSE

Chief Executive Officer & Managing Principal, Cherry Hill Advisory

Hi Hammad,

54% of audit and risk professionals cite AI governance and model risk as the area their organization is least prepared to address in 2026. (Source: Cherry Hill Advisory, “Navigating 2026: Top Emerging Risks for Audit, Risk, and Compliance” presentation delivered as a Wolters Kluwer TeamMate webinar, n=3,349). Not cybersecurity. Not regulatory complexity. AI governance.

Meanwhile, employees are adopting AI tools faster than governance structures can form around them, creating a growing layer of unmanaged risk that internal audit, IT compliance, and risk functions have limited visibility into. The gap between AI adoption velocity and organizational oversight is not a technology problem. It is a governance problem. This session provides a structured approach to closing that gap. Attendees will assess where shadow AI exposure exists within their organizations, apply a practical governance framework that integrates AI risk into existing enterprise risk management and internal control structures, and evaluate how internal audit can provide meaningful assurance over AI. The focus is operational: what to implement, how to scope it, and where to start.

Learning Objectives:
*Assess the primary risk categories created by unmanaged AI adoption, including data exposure, model reliability, and regulatory noncompliance, and identify where those risks intersect with existing governance and control structures.

*Evaluate internal audit, risk management, and IT Compliance's role in providing assurance over AI governance, including how to scope AI audit engagements, define testable control objectives, and integrate AI risk into the annual audit plan without creating a standalone compliance layer.

SPEAKER:

Jim Pelletier CIA, CGAP

Lead Product Manager

Wolters Kluwer TeamMate

Hi Hammad,

Organizations often struggle with fragmented governance, risk, and compliance (GRC) functions, leading to duplicated efforts, inconsistent risk visibility, and missed opportunities for strategic alignment. As risks become more interconnected and internal audit functions face increasing pressure to do more with limited resources, the need for a coordinated GRC approach has never been greater.

This session explores how internal audit can act as a catalyst for breaking down silos between audit, risk management, and compliance teams. Drawing on current industry research and practical use cases, attendees will learn how to foster collaboration, streamline processes, and leverage integrated GRC approaches to enhance organizational resilience. The session will highlight how aligning these functions improves efficiency, strengthens risk oversight, and supports better decision-making across the enterprise.

Learning Objectives:
*Understand the impact of siloed GRC functions in the context of today’s interconnected risk landscape and resource constraints.
*Identify the core elements of an integrated GRC approach and the evolving role of internal audit in driving alignment.
*Apply practical strategies to improve collaboration, enhance risk visibility, and optimize assurance efforts across GRC functions.

Join us Thursday, May 14, 2026, at 11:00 AM EDT for a free 1-hour webinar. Participants will earn 1 CPE credit.

We hope to see you there!
